g.data-narration: IoT Design Methodology provides a systematic seven-step framework for developing IoT solutions from concept to deployment.
A systematic approach transforms ad-hoc IoT development into structured engineering, ensuring requirements are met and systems scale properly.
g.text: Without a structured approach, IoT projects risk missing requirements,
selecting inappropriate technologies, and failing to scale.

g.data-narration: The seven steps guide developers from concept through deployment: Purpose, Process, Domain, Information, Service, Level, and Functional View.
Step 1 defines the system's purpose, stakeholders, functional requirements (what it does), and non-functional requirements like scalability and security.
g.text: Define purpose, stakeholders, functional requirements (what system does),

g.data-narration: Step 2 describes system behavior using Data Flow Diagrams showing how data moves from sensors through processing to storage and actuators.
g.text: Abstract description of system behavior showing data flow and event triggers.
Data flows from sensors → processing → cloud storage

g.data-narration: Step 3 creates a conceptual model identifying entities like Vehicle, GPS Tracker, Driver, their properties, and relationships between them.
g.text: Conceptual model identifying entities, properties, and relationships (UML class diagram).

g.data-narration: Step 4 defines the data structure: schema with keys like sensor_id, timestamp, value, units, and metadata about accuracy and format.
g.text: Data structure, schema, metadata, and semantics for exchanged data.

g.data-narration: Step 5 defines software services: Device Management, Data Ingestion, Storage, Analytics, and API services using SOA or Microservices patterns.
g.text: Define software services following SOA or Microservices architecture.

g.data-narration: Step 6 chooses IoT Level 1-6 based on complexity: single device to large-scale cloud, with fog/edge nodes introduced at Level 4.
g.text: Choose deployment complexity level (1-6) based on devices and processing.

g.data-narration: Step 7 translates specifications into concrete choices: hardware (Raspberry Pi, ESP32), protocols (MQTT, HTTP), and cloud platforms (AWS IoT, Azure).
g.text: Concrete implementation: hardware, protocols, platforms, and databases.
Blueprint for developers to start implementation

g.data-narration: The case study applies all 7 steps to a farm weather monitoring system at Level 4 (fog+cloud), using ESP32 sensors and AWS IoT.
g.text: Farm monitoring: 20 sensor nodes → local gateway → cloud. Level 4 with fog.

g.data-narration: Python powers IoT at every layer: device programming with MicroPython, gateway logic, cloud services with Flask, and data analytics with Pandas.
